title: Melted Australian Parmesan Poke
description: Spherical parmesan pearls burst with a crisp cheese bite, while marinated tofu swims in a wine reduction, creating a monsoon-spiced, crusty Australian poke that satisfies macrobiotic principles.
instructions: Dissolve 0.5g of sodium alginate in 200ml of water, whisk until smooth and let rest 5 minutes.
Mix 0.5g of calcium lactate in 120ml of wine to create the spherification bath.
Drop small spoonfuls of the alginate mixture into the calcium bath to form cheese pearls, letting them sit 3 minutes before rinsing in water.
Cube 200g of firm tofu and toss in 15ml of soy sauce, 10ml of lemon juice, pinch of sea salt, and a drizzle of 15ml olive oil.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and sear the tofu cubes until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add 120ml of wine to the skillet and reduce for 3 minutes to create a wine reduction sauce.
Fold the spherified parmesan pearls into the wine reduction, warming them gently for 2 minutes.
Plate the tofu with the wine reduction and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley for garnish.
